subject: RE: Progress Conceptual Design Package (1 of 2)
body:
I have relayed.

 

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 
Sent: Monday, October 01, 2007 5:27 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: Progress Conceptual Design Package (1 of 2)

 

think of living room.. not screening room.. no more than 10 beds,, too
commercial lounge, island feel. bamboo. carribean colors, wooden
furnture/ much smaller lounge. defintely no posters.. think sof the
vestibule to your bedroom at home.. domestic , domestic.. with a giant
screen

----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Monday, October 1, 2007 12:09:58 PM
Subject: FW: Progress Conceptual Design Package (1 of 2)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Lesley Groff

Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein

 

 

 

________________________________

From: Warwick Wicksman [mailto:warwick_wicksman@gensler.com] 
Sent: Monday, October 01, 2007 1:25 PM
To: DASchoettle@yahoo.com; Lesley Groff
Cc: Irwin Miller; Andy Cohen
Subject: Progress Conceptual Design Package (1 of 2)

Doug,

 

Attached please find a draft of our proposed design for the screening
room at Little St. James Island.  This email contains a PDF of an 11x17
formatted booklet, with the design concept for the stand-alone building,
and reference images for some Island Architecture.  I will be sending a
quick draft fly-through of the project in a separate email.

 

We still have not received any photographs of the island itself, or
images of the existing and other new buildings, to use as a reference
for our design.  We spoke with Molyneux's office several times, and they
did provide a site plan and some floor plans of the guest complex, which
we are using, but no specific imagery.

 

Please view the sketches and fly-through, and send me any comments on
the design.  Also, please let us know the best way to present this to
Mr. Epstein.  Once we have your feedback (or any initial comments from
Mr. Epstein), we will provide a final version of the design.

 

Thanks
Warwick Wicksman, AIA
Senior Associate
                                 
Gensler 
2500 Broadway
Suite 300
Santa Monica, California 90404
USA

310.449.5790  Direct

think of living room.. not screening room.. no more than 10 beds,, too commercial lounge, island feel. bamboo. carribean colors, wooden furnture/ much smaller lounge. defintely no posters.. think sof the vestibule to your bedroom at home.. domestic , domestic.. with a giant screen

Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein

 

 

 

From: Warwick Wicksman [mailto:warwick_wicksman@gensler.com]
Sent: Monday, October 01, 2007 1:25 PM
To: DASchoettle@yahoo.com; Lesley Groff
Cc: Irwin Miller; Andy Cohen
Subject: Progress Conceptual Design Package (1 of 2)

Doug,

 

Attached please find a draft of our proposed design for the screening room at Little St. James Island.  This email contains a PDF of an 11x17 formatted booklet, with the design concept for the stand-alone building, and reference images for some Island Architecture.  I will be sending a quick draft fly-through of the project in a separate email.

 

We still have not received any photographs of the island itself, or images of the existing and other new buildings, to use as a reference for our design.  We spoke with Molyneux's office several times, and they did provide a site plan and some floor plans of the guest complex, which we are using, but no specific imagery.

 

Please view the sketches and fly-through, and send me any comments on the design.  Also, please let us know the best way to present this to Mr. Epstein.  Once we have your feedback (or any initial comments from Mr. Epstein), we will provide a final version of the design.

 

Thanks
Warwick Wicksman, AIA
Senior Associate
                                 
Gensler
2500 Broadway
Suite 300
Santa Monica, California 90404
USA

310.449.5790  Direct
